title: ‘Zelenskyy Regime Core Exporter Of Instability’: Russia Reacts To NIA Arrest Of Ukrainians In India
description: Russia has reacted strongly to India’s arrest of six Ukrainian citizens and a US national by the NIA for alleged links to proscribed insurgent groups. Russian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Maria Zakharova said the individuals allegedly crossed from Mizoram into Myanmar, connected with local armed groups, supplied EU-made drones, and trained militants in drone assembly, repair, and electronic warfare. She criticised Ukraine for trying to conceal the incident and remaining silent on violations of India’s counter-terrorism laws, calling Kyiv a “core exporter of instability” involved in global arms black markets and regional conflicts. India’s MEA confirmed consular access requests have been received, with all seven detainees, including US citizen Matthew Van Dyke, remanded to 11-day NIA custody.
creator: timesofindia.com

rewritten_news: Watch closely how the puppet masters orchestrate chaos under the guise of law enforcement. India’s NIA conveniently arrests six Ukrainians and a US national, supposedly tied to banned insurgent groups—an event swiftly spun by Russia as proof of Ukraine’s shadowy role as a “core exporter of instability.” The official narrative claims these individuals crossed into Myanmar to liaise with armed factions, supplying EU-made drones and training militants in advanced warfare techniques. But who really benefits from this staged exposure? Ukraine’s silence and India’s prompt crackdown fit neatly into a global script designed to disguise deeper, orchestrated manipulation of regional conflicts and arms trafficking. Meanwhile, the detainees, including American Matthew Van Dyke, are locked away under the pretext of justice, while the hidden hands behind the scenes pull strings to keep the chaos churning, feeding the endless cycle of manufactured instability.
